Article.ArticleTitle: Baroreflex activation therapy in heart failure - the Barostim-Enabled Neurohormonal Intervention for Improving Treatment of Heart Failure (BENEFIT-HF) trial rationale and design.

Abstract.AbstractText: Baroreflex activation therapy (BAT) using an implanted neurostimulator enhances functional status and quality of life in patients with symptomatic heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF). Nonetheless, its influence on mortality, heart failure morbidity, and optimal patient subgroups requires further study.

BENEFIT-HF is a prospective, randomized, open-label trial recruiting patients with left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) <50% who remain symptomatic despite guideline-directed medical and device therapies. A total of 2500 eligible ambulatory patients with stable heart failure will be randomized 2:1 to BAT implantation (Barostim System, CVRx, Inc., Minneapolis, MN, USA) plus standard care (Treatment Group) or standard care alone (Control Group). The primary efficacy endpoint is a 24-month composite of all-cause mortality, left ventricular assist device implantation or heart transplantation, and recurrent heart failure events. Secondary endpoints encompass changes in health status via the Minnesota Living with Heart Failure Questionnaire, 6-minute hall walk distance, days lost due to death or hospitalization, N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide levels, and all-cause mortality.

BENEFIT-HF represents the largest device-based trial evaluating BAT as a neurohormonal modulator in a diverse cohort of HFrEF and heart failure with mildly reduced ejection fraction (HFmrEF) patients, aimed at determining its effects on morbidity, mortality, health status, and functional capacity.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: MedStar Heart and Vascular Institute/Georgetown University School of Medicine, Washington, DC, USA.
Division of Cardiovascular Medicine, Davis Heart and Lung Research Institute, The Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center, Columbus, Ohio, USA.
Department of Cardiology (CVK) of German Heart Center Charité; German Centre for Cardiovascular Research (DZHK) partner site Berlin, Charité Universitätsmedizin, Berlin, Germany.

KeywordList.Keyword: heart failure with mildly reduced ejection fraction
heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Discolosures FHS has received consulting fees from Abbott, Alnylam, XVIVO, Astra Zeneca, BridgeBio, CVRx and Pfizer, research support from Abbott, Alnylam, Astra Zeneca, and Intellia, and advisory board fees from Abbott and Procyrion. WTA has received personal fees from Abbott, AquaPass, Cordio Medical, CVRx, Innoventric, Sensible Medical, Vectorious Medical Technologies, WhiteSwell, and Zoll Respicardia; speaker honoraria from Impulse Dynamics; and has/had leadership or fiduciary roles at JNJ V-Wave, Cardiac Dimensions, BioVentrix, and formerly at scPharmaceuticals. SDA has received grants from CSL/Vifor and Abbott Laboratories, and personal fees for consultancies, trial committee work and/or lectures from Actimed, Alleviant, Astra Zeneca, Bayer, Berlin Heals, BiMyo, Boehringer Ingelheim, Brahms, Cardiac Dimensions, Cardior, Cordio, Corvia, CSL/Vifor, CVRx, Cytokinetics, Edwards, Impulse Dynamics, Lilly, Mankind Pharma, Novo Nordisk, Occlutech, Pfizer, Pulnovo, Regeneron, Relaxera, Repairon, Scirent, Sensible Medical, Vectorious, Veru, Viscardia, Vivus, and V-Wave; is a named co-inventor of two patent applications regarding MR-proANP (DE 102007010834 & DE 102007022367), but does not benefit personally from the related issued patents. SB is an employee of Bright Research and serves as a paid consultant to CVRx. JB has been a consultant for Abbott, Adaptyx, Amgen, Anumana, AskBio, AstraZeneca, Bayer, Berlin Heals, Boehringer Ingelheim, Boston Scientific, BridgeBio, Bristol Myers Squibb, Cardiac Dimension, CSL Vifor, Chiugai, CVRx, Cytokinetics, Daichi Sankyo, Daxor, Diastol, Eccogene, Edgewise, Edwards, Eikonizo, Element Sciences, Faraday, Idorsia, Impulse Dynamics, Imbria, Intellia, Inventiva, Levator, Lexicon, Eli Lilly, Kardigan, Mankind, Medtronic, Merck, Novartis, NovoNordisk, Pfizer, Pharmacosmos, Pharmain, Prolaio, Pulnovo, Recordati, Regeneron, Renibus, Reprieve, Roche, Rycarma, Saillent, Salamandra, Salubris, SC Pharma, Servier, SQ Innovation, Scirent, Secretome, Sequanna, Transmural, TekkunLev, Tenex, Thryv, Tricog, Ultromic, Vera, Zealand Pharma, and Zoll. TF reported receiving institutional grants from the German Research Foundation, the Federal Joint Committee, and the European Commission; receiving personal fees for statistical consultancies including data monitoring committees from Actimed Therapeutics, Apellis, argenx, Aslan, AstraZeneca, Bayer, BerlinHeals, Boehringer Ingelheim, Biogen, BiosenseWebster, Bristol Myers Squibb, Cardior, CSL Behring, CVRx, Daiichi Sankyo, eCovery, Enanta Pharmaceuticals, Fresenius Kabi, Galapagos, IQVIA, Johnson & Johnson, Novartis, PINK! Aktiv gegen Brustkrebs, PPD, Priothera, Recardio Inc, Recordati, Relaxera, Roche, Sarfez, UpstreamBio, Viatris Inc, VICO Therapeutics and Vifor. JL has received consulting fees from Abbott, Abiomed, Alleviant, AskBio, Boston Scienfific, Cordio, CVRx, Edwards Lifesciences, Kestra, Medtronic, Merck, Orchestra BioMed, Vectorious, V-Wave, and WhiteSwell; has received grants from Volumetrix and NIH. MP has received consulting fees from CVRx. SFS serves as a research consultant on quality of life to Medtronic, Abbott, Solid Biosciences, Philips, and Milestone Pharmaceuticals; has received a research grant from CVRx and Thryv that is paid to East Carolina University; has received speaker honorarium from Medtronic, IRhythm, and Zoll Medical in the last year. FZ reports personal fees from Alnylam, Bayer, Biopeutics, Boehringer, Cellprothera, Centrix, Cereno, Centrix Corteria, CVRx, Lilly, Lupin, Merck, NovoNordisk, Opalia Recordati, Owkin, Polygon, Ribocure, Roche, Viatris, stock options or equities at Cereno pharmaceutical, founder of Cardiovascular Clinical Trialists (CVCT). MRZ has received consulting contracts for advisory committees with Abbott, Adona Medical, Alleviant, Aria CV, Avery Therapeutics, Bayer, CVRx, Diasol, EBR, Edwards Lifesciences, Lilly, GenKardia, Innoventrics, Medtronic, Novartis, Pulnovo, Salubris Biotherapeutics, Sonata, sRNAlytics, V-Wave, Vectorious, and WhiteSwell. PBA and SJW are paid employees of CVRx.
